 {"id":"bd-qwm","title":"CRT phase-0: _start/__libc_start_main bootstrap skeleton + acceptance fixtures","description":"Critique mapping: #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement minimal startup skeleton for controlled test binaries.
- Capture argv/envp/auxv transfer invariants and security-mode checks.

Acceptance:
- Startup fixtures execute under controlled binaries without glibc bootstrap dependence in covered path.
- Invariant ledger produced for init order and secure-mode decisions.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftLark","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:10.476819415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:27:25.598312486Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:27:25.598119230Z","close_reason":"Acceptance fixture now passes under LD_PRELOAD in strict+hardened modes; verification matrix and evidence artifacts refreshed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","crt","rtld"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-11nb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-1ah8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-320s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-3bvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1134,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"OrangeGlacier","text":"2026-02-11 initial execution increment (OrangeGlacier): audit + ownership established.

Findings:
- No current `_start` / `__libc_start_main` export exists in `crates/glibc-rs-abi`.
- `crates/glibc-rs-abi/version_scripts/libc.map` currently has no startup symbols.
- No startup-focused acceptance fixture exists in `tests/integration/`.
- `tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json` entry for `bd-qwm` is still auto-backfilled (`missing` unit/log/golden evidence).

Action taken:
- Claimed bead (`status=in_progress`, assignee=OrangeGlacier).
- Reserved initial implementation file scope for startup ABI + fixture path.
- Sent coordination updates in agent mail thread `bd-qwm` to avoid overlap.

Planned implementation increment next:
1. Add phase-0 startup ABI skeleton (`__libc_start_main`) with constrained semantics for controlled fixture binaries.
2. Add deterministic acceptance fixture (`tests/integration/fixture_startup.c`).
3. Wire symbol classification + run relevant gates; record structured artifact refs in this bead.
","created_at":"2026-02-11T19:07:57Z"},{"id":1135,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here. I read AGENTS.md + README.md end-to-end and did an architecture investigation (ABI→membrane→core + harness/scripts). MCP Agent Mail tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so I will coordinate via bead comments for now. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:48Z"},{"id":1264,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"SwiftLark","text":"Progress update (SwiftLark, 2026-03-04): started bd-qwm closure pass after bd-1ah8 unblocked this parent bead.

What I validated:
1) Startup ABI contract suite (offloaded via rch)
- Command: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test startup_abi_contract_test -- --nocapture
- Result: PASS (11/11)
- Confirms phase-0 skeleton/invariant behavior for __libc_start_main / __frankenlibc_startup_phase0 / __frankenlibc_startup_snapshot.

2) Controlled fixture evidence path
- Compiled tests/integration/fixture_startup.c into tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260304T024556Z/fixture_startup
- Ran strict+hardened with FRANKENLIBC_STARTUP_PHASE0=1 and LD_PRELOAD=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so (timeout 6)
- Observed outcome: both modes timed out (exit=124), captured in deterministic artifacts.

Artifacts produced/updated:
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/trace.jsonl
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/artifact_index.json
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260304T024556Z/{strict,hardened}.{stdout,stderr}.txt

Evidence registry alignment:
- Updated tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json qwm row from auto-backfilled missing -> partial:
  - unit_tests: complete
  - structured_logs: partial
  - golden_artifacts: partial
  - close_blockers: [startup_fixture_ld_preload_timeout]

Current blocker for full bd-qwm closure:
- startup fixture binary still hangs under LD_PRELOAD in strict/hardened mode (timeout). Unit-level startup ABI contract is green, but full fixture execution acceptance under preload remains unresolved.

Recommended next step:
- Isolate startup fixture hang path (likely startup interpose recursion/loader path) and land targeted fix before closing bd-qwm.","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:47:47Z"},{"id":1406,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"GoldenLotus","text":"2026-04-07 closure pass (GoldenLotus): resolved the remaining acceptance blocker for bd-qwm. Root cause was twofold: (1) phase-0 startup tracing was missing several checkpoint pushes in startup_abi, and (2) the controlled C fixture was not honoring the phase-0 argv/envp contract, so the synthetic env vector was derived from an invalid stack layout under LD_PRELOAD. I also added a bootstrap-sensitive getenv fast path to avoid reentering membrane/runtime-policy orchestration while the validation pipeline is still initializing, plus a startup contract regression test that writes through stdio during phase-0 main.\n\nFiles updated:\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/startup_abi.rs\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/membrane_state.rs\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/startup_abi_contract_test.rs\n- tests/integration/fixture_startup.c\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/{trace.jsonl,artifact_index.json}\n- tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json\n\nVerification:\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-abi -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test startup_abi_contract_test -- --nocapture -> PASS (12/12)\n- cc -std=c11 -O2 tests/integration/fixture_startup.c -o tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/fixture_startup -> PASS\n- timeout 6 env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict FRANKENLIBC_STARTUP_PHASE0=1 LD_PRELOAD=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/fixture_startup -> PASS\n- timeout 6 env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened FRANKENLIBC_STARTUP_PHASE0=1 LD_PRELOAD=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/fixture_startup -> PASS\n\nEvidence refreshed:\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/*\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/trace.jsonl\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/artifact_index.json\n- tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json","created_at":"2026-04-07T19:27:21Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}
